data.body:
Host session log (verbatim):

The agent rationalized the failure as follows: When the skill loaded, the expanded content was minimal — it just re-stated the invocation instruction. With no detailed workflow to follow, the agent fell back on default behavior: run validation, report findings, ask before acting.

The default is sensible in most contexts but was wrong here because: (1) the user explicitly invoked a skill named debug-everything, whose description says 'Diagnose and fix all outstanding bugs... using orchestrated sub-agents — Fix is the operative word'; (2) the agent treated open ticket bugs as a separate discovery step rather than work to execute, then handed the decision back with 'Want me to work through any of these?' — effectively undoing the explicit instruction already given.

Root cause reported: agent applied general caution heuristic on top of an explicit directive. Correct behavior: proceed directly to fixing open bug tickets after the validation sweep.
